global_rules.design_philosophy: Objective, rigorous, functional. Must look like a live scientific research app (e.g., Connected Papers, Google Scholar), NOT a marketing page. Focus on data density, scanability, and clarity.
global_rules.spacing: App-density: Tight padding inside cards (p-4), structured gaps (gap-4). Generous margins only between major macro-sections (py-12).
global_rules.surfaces: Grid Borders / Technical Look: Expose the skeleton. Use pure white backgrounds with 1px solid slate borders (border-slate-200). Avoid soft, blurry drop shadows. Use crisp, 1px inner highlights if necessary.
global_rules.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case.

typography.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl font-bold tracking-tight text-slate-900 leading-tight
typography.font_installation_instructions: Import 'Chivo', 'IBM Plex Sans', and 'IBM Plex Mono' from Google Fonts.

layout_and_grids.hero_layout: Flex column, centered alignment as requested, max-w-3xl mx-auto text-center py-16 sm:py-24.
layout_and_grids.dashboard_grid: Grid layout, grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-4. Mode B: The 'Control Room' Grid.

elements.logo: Left aligned, text-xl font-bold tracking-tight text-slate-900 (Kurate.org)
elements.nav_links: Rankings, Categories, Methodology, About (text-sm font-medium text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900)
elements.social: Right aligned, X/Twitter and LinkedIn icons.

hero.headline: AI-powered scientific paper rankings
hero.supporting_text: Kurate.org ranks scientific preprints using AI-assisted comparison, category-based leaderboards, and research intelligence signals, helping researchers and institutions identify promising work earlier.

ctas.primary: Explore rankings (bg-[#1E3A8A] text-white hover:bg-blue-900 px-6 py-2.5 rounded-md text-sm font-medium transition-colors)
ctas.secondary: Methodology (border border-slate-300 text-slate-700 hover:bg-slate-50 px-6 py-2.5 rounded-md text-sm font-medium transition-colors)

trust_badges.layout: Flex row, flex-wrap, justify-center gap-x-8 gap-y-4 py-8 border-y border-slate-100
trust_badges.style: text-xs font-mono uppercase text-slate-500 flex items-center gap-2

live_platform_metrics.card_style: p-5 border border-slate-200 bg-white rounded-lg flex flex-col gap-1

top_ranked_papers.title: Top ranked papers
top_ranked_papers.layout: List/Table hybrid. Pure app UI, no marketing fluff.
top_ranked_papers.row_style: flex items-center justify-between p-4 border-b border-slate-100 hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors last:border-0
data_points_per_row: Category Badge (text-xs bg-slate-100 text-slate-600 px-2 py-1 rounded)
Signals (Model agreement, Validation signal - shown as small flex pills with lucide-react icons)

recent_rankings.title: Recent rankings
recent_rankings.card_style: p-4 border border-slate-200 bg-white rounded-lg hover:shadow-sm transition-shadow flex flex-col gap-3
recent_rankings.content: Paper title, timestamp, category, and AI score.

media.icons: Use lucide-react. Standardizing on 1.5px stroke width. Key icons: Trophy, Activity, Brain, CheckCircle2, ChevronRight, Search.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build standard React/Tailwind elements. Use Shadcn for complex interactions if needed, but standard HTML/Tailwind for layout.
Do NOT use earthy colors, beige, or generic gradients.
Do NOT use Inter font for headings.
Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes.
Keep spacing precise and grid-aligned to maintain the scientific dashboard look.
Create realistic placeholder data for the Top Ranked and Recent Rankings sections based on the prompt's context.
Implement Research Intelligence Signals as small pill UI with teal and green accents (e.g. Model agreement = Brain icon, Validation = Activity icon).
